metadata.from: Bob (Robert) Hanifen — signed 'Bobby', writing to his father
metadata.to: James Edward Hanifen Sr. (Dad) — addressed to Hanifen Body & Paint Co.
metadata.location_written_from: Chanute Field, Illinois (based on return address: Sfd. T BKS-249, Chanute Fld. Ill.)
metadata.location_sent_to: Hanifen Body & Paint Co., 904 Neo Way, Des Moines, Iowa
metadata.envelope_notes: Return address: Pvt. Bob Hanifen 11176600, Sfd. T BKS-249, Chanute Fld. Ill. Marked 'Free' in upper right corner (military free mail). Addressed to Hanifen Body & Paint Co., 904 Neo Way, Des Moines, Iowa.
topics: plans to come home this week
asking about the '37 Plymouth and new motor
selling rides to Iowa City for profit
possible occupation army duty in the Islands
metadata.mood_tone: Upbeat and enthusiastic about the weekend trip; conversational and warm toward his father; some underlying uncertainty about postwar assignments.
metadata.legibility_notes: Handwriting is generally readable but some words are difficult — 'Melanie' or similar name on page 2 is uncertain; 'Neo Way' on envelope may be misread; page 3 has a crossed-out word; 'Sfd. T' on envelope is unclear abbreviation. The name of the buddy's aunt/host and 'Melanie' are phonetic guesses.
metadata.historical_context: Written in August 1945, near the end of WWII. Bob mentions uncertainty about how the war ending will affect him, and fears he may be assigned two years in 'the Islands' as part of an occupation army. This reflects the real concern of U.S. servicemen in mid-1945 about Pacific occupation duty following Japan's surrender.

pages.transcription:
Mon, 13
time 6:30

U. S. ARMY AIR FORCES

Dear Dad
  Just got back from a wonderful weekend in Peoria, with my buddie Hank. We stayed at his aunt house over the weekend. We left camp at 2:00 and rode into Champaign in a trailer and it rained all the way. We started hitchhiking at 3:00 and at 8:00 we finally got a ride out of time. Arrived in Peoria around 11:00.

U. S. ARMY AIR FORCES

Hank cousin took us to church at 12:00, came back and went out to the golf courses to Melanie's [?] father, came back for dinner, had a swell feed, took a 2 hr. nap and Melanie [?] fixed us up with some girls. We all went on a picnic had a swell time, seen a lot of good golf at the golf courses, lot of Pros were there for a big tournament. Joe Kirkwood the trick shot artist was

U. S. ARMY AIR FORCES

there, He put on a [crossed out word] swell show. We then went to the show and got to bed around 1:00 never got up till 10:30 had a nice breakfast and left for the fld. got back at 4:30.
  I'm going to come home this week if everything goes right. How is the 37 Ply, did you ever put in that new motor. This guy is going to Ft. Daly [?] but he is not coming back. I come make expense and some profit by picking

U. S. ARMY AIR FORCES

up four guys in Iowa City, 2 bucks apiece. Write and tell me all about it will you. Well Dad goodbye for now. And I will write again real soon. I don't know what the war ending will affect me. Probably will get 2 years in the Islands as occupation army. Sure hope not. So Dad I see your
  Son Bobby

full_transcription:
Mon, 13
time 6:30

Dear Dad
  Just got back from a wonderful weekend in Peoria, with my buddie Hank. We stayed at his aunt house over the weekend. We left camp at 2:00 and rode into Champaign in a trailer and it rained all the way. We started hitchhiking at 3:00 and at 8:00 we finally got a ride out of time. Arrived in Peoria around 11:00.

Hank cousin took us to church at 12:00, came back and went out to the golf courses to Melanie's [?] father, came back for dinner, had a swell feed, took a 2 hr. nap and Melanie [?] fixed us up with some girls. We all went on a picnic had a swell time, seen a lot of good golf at the golf courses, lot of Pros were there for a big tournament. Joe Kirkwood the trick shot artist was

there, He put on a [crossed out word] swell show. We then went to the show and got to bed around 1:00 never got up till 10:30 had a nice breakfast and left for the fld. got back at 4:30.
  I'm going to come home this week if everything goes right. How is the 37 Ply, did you ever put in that new motor. This guy is going to Ft. Daly [?] but he is not coming back. I come make expense and some profit by picking

up four guys in Iowa City, 2 bucks apiece. Write and tell me all about it will you. Well Dad goodbye for now. And I will write again real soon. I don't know what the war ending will affect me. Probably will get 2 years in the Islands as occupation army. Sure hope not. So Dad I see your
  Son Bobby
summary: Written on Monday, August 13, 1945, from Chanute Field, Illinois, Bob Hanifen writes to his father at the family auto body business in Des Moines to describe a wonderful weekend pass to Peoria with his Army buddy Hank. The two hitchhiked to Peoria, stayed at Hank's aunt's house, attended church, visited a golf tournament featuring trick-shot artist Joe Kirkwood, went on a picnic with girls, and saw a show. Bob mentions plans to come home that week, asks about a '37 Plymouth and its new motor, and notes he can make some money picking up riders to Iowa City. The letter closes with Bob expressing uncertainty about his postwar fate, worrying he may be assigned two years of occupation duty 'in the Islands' following the war's end.
